event_blocks.summary: The scene opens with a dramatic orchestral and electronic score accompanying the 'MARVEL RISING PLAYING WITH FIRE' title sequence, transitioning to ambient museum sounds of light chatter and footsteps within the 'TREASURES OF THE ANCIENTS' exhibit. A girl with long black hair, wearing a teal jacket and pink scarf, animatedly gestures with her smartphone, explaining to a boy with reddish-brown hair, clad in a black jacket over a red hoodie, that ancient ancestors worked with precious metals and gemstones, their artistry revealing much about their culture. The boy, looking concerned, asks if she is checking something or watching again, to which the girl defensively replies she is staying vigilant for heroes needing help, expressing frustration that things have been boring with villains on vacation and wishing for a J-Walker or similar threat. The boy, now smiling, states he is enjoying the downtime, having reached level 10 on Cosmic Blaster and tried every vegan donut flavor, advising to skip Basil Rhubarb Crunch. The girl, looking at her phone with disbelief and enthusiasm, exclaims that Basil Rhubarb Crunch is amazing and hands the phone to the boy, as a woman with long dark hair and yellow-framed glasses observes their interaction with a serious expression amidst the museum's classical architecture and natural light.

raw_segments: The minute opens with a dramatic, heroic orchestral and electronic score accompanying a title sequence featuring the bold red 'MARVEL' logo, followed by 'RISING' emerging from a fiery, pixelated explosion, and 'PLAYING WITH FIRE' in a bold orange font. The music then softens, giving way to ambient museum sounds of light chatter and footsteps as the scene transitions to a well-lit museum exhibit titled 'TREASURES OF THE ANCIENTS'. A diverse group of visitors, including children and adults, observe ancient artifacts displayed in glass cases, with the camera panning across relics and a large black cat statue. A girl with long black hair, dressed in a teal jacket and pink scarf, gestures animatedly while holding a smartphone, speaking energetically about how "Our ancestors thousands of years ago were working with precious metals and gemstones," and how the "artistry... tells us a lot about their culture." A boy with reddish-brown hair, in a black jacket over a red hoodie, listens with a slightly concerned expression, then asks calmly, "Are you checking here or watch again?" The girl, in a defensive and rapid-fire tone, explains she's "just staying vigilant," seeing if "there are any heroes we need help?" She then expresses frustration that "things have been boring lately," with "every villain seems to be on vacation," lamenting, "We can't do that without bad guys? Give me a J-Walker, something!" The boy, now smiling and gesturing, responds in a relaxed, content voice that he's "digging the downtime," having reached "level 10 on Cosmic Blaster" and tried "every flavor at the vegan donut shop," advising with a hint of disgust to "Skip Basil Rhubarb Crunch... weird." The girl, looking at her phone and making a dismissive gesture, reacts with disbelief and enthusiasm, declaring, "Serious? Basil Rhubarb Crunch is amazing!" as she hands the phone to the boy. The scene ends with a close-up of a woman with long dark hair and yellow-framed glasses, wearing a brown vest over a blue shirt, observing the interaction with a serious expression, all within the museum's classical architecture and natural light.

event_blocks.summary: Inside a warmly lit museum hall displaying ancient artifacts like a black cat statue and a golden headdress under a 'TREASURES OF THE ANCIENTS' banner, a female guide's voice prompted visitors about a potential quiz, eliciting groans and sarcastic remarks about their enthusiasm for history before describing the centerpiece: a priceless solid gold crown with platinum inlays and 100 precious stones. The Girl with long black hair, wearing a teal jacket, showed a text message to the Boy with reddish-brown hair, in a black jacket, reading 'I'm in town. We NEED to talk.' As a tense, magical sound built, the Girl asked, 'Everything okay?' and the Boy replied, 'Yeah, it's fine.' Suddenly, the lighting shifted to an urgent red, and with a whoosh and power-up sound, both transformed: the Girl became Ms. Marvel in a blue and red costume with a lightning bolt emblem and red cape, excitedly exclaiming, 'Woohoo! Finally, an emergency!', while the Boy became Inferno in a black and red suit with a circular emblem, hesitantly suggesting, 'Maybe we should let museum security handle it.' Ms. Marvel dismissed this, stating, 'No way! Secret Warriors coming in hot!', as Inferno sheepishly added he was 'just workshopping it.' A comedic struggle followed, with Ms. Marvel exclaiming, 'Hold on! My arm's caught in the sleeve!' accompanied by a grunt and a 'boom' sound. Striking heroic poses, they confronted a surprised Woman in a denim jacket and glasses, with Ms. Marvel confidently declaring, 'Where's the bad guy? He doesn't stand a chance now that Ms. Marvel and Inferno are here!', to which the startled Woman tentatively responded, 'Um, hi. I think maybe...'

event_blocks.summary: Inside the 'TREASURES OF THE ANCIENTS' museum hall, the Boy with reddish-brown hair and the Girl with long black hair, both in superhero costumes, confront the Woman with a denim jacket and glasses, identified as Zayla, a field trip participant. Zayla explains she got too close to a display, and the Boy, suspecting villainy, questions her presence, but Zayla clarifies she's part of the group. The Girl asks America (presumably herself) for her opinion on being normal, to which Zayla, still unimpressed, states she wasn't listening.

raw_segments: The minute opens in a grand museum hall, labeled 'TREASURES OF THE ANCIENTS,' where the Boy with reddish-brown hair, in his superhero costume, stands with a stern expression as the Girl with long black hair, also in her superheroine costume, points accusingly at the Woman with a denim jacket and glasses. The Woman, initially surprised, responds with a calm female voice, stating, "I just got a little too close to the display." The Boy, with a young male voice, presses, "You sure you didn't see anyone else? Maybe a villain engaged in nefarious misdeeds?" The Woman laughs, then introduces herself as "Zayla" with a calm voice, explaining, "Yeah, no. I'm Zayla, I'm a part of the field trip. Sorry." The Boy then tells her to "Catch up with the class." The Girl, with a young female voice, questions Zayla, "Who exactly are you supposed to be?" and then, "Hey, aren't you in my class?" The Boy quickly interjects, "Nah, no, no, he's no one. He's just someone out there fighting crime. You should really pay attention to the teacher. Eyes forward, young lady!" The scene transitions to an exterior shot of a brick building with a sign reading 'WILSON'S AUTO,' then inside the dim, industrial lighting of the auto shop. The Boy and Girl, now in civilian clothes, walk in and approach Zayla, who is now working on a green bicycle frame, wearing a red jacket with yellow stars and a dog tag necklace, appearing unimpressed. The Girl asks, "Do you think we're really having a quiz on the museum exhibit?" The Boy responds, "If so, cool with me. All about the normal stuff, remember?" The Girl, gesturing, declares, "I used to believe the hype about normal, but it is so overrated. My life now blows it out of the water." The Boy replies, "I'm pretty sure you were always above average." The Girl laughs and asks, "America, what do you think? Being normal? Yes or who needs it?" revealing her name. Zayla, still displaying a serious and unimpressed demeanor, responds, "I'm sorry. I wasn't listening to what..."

event_blocks.summary: In Wilson's Auto Shop, the Boy with reddish-brown hair and the Girl with long black hair, Gwen, engaged in a serious conversation punctuated by text message notifications from Gwen's pink smartphone; Gwen expressed frustration over the frequent texts, which the Boy dismissed as usual, while Gwen explained they were from her mom due to her absence with the Secret Warriors. Doreen, the Woman with long dark hair and yellow glasses, joined them skeptically as Gwen playfully reassured the Boy, referring to him as 'Yummy' and 'Smiley face,' before activating a device on her jacket, revealing a glowing red emblem with a lightning bolt, which opened a portal. They stepped into a futuristic, circular chamber with a target design on the floor, and upon exiting, found themselves in a high-tech, multi-level recreational space with a climbing wall and a large screen. The Boy and Gwen sat on a ledge, while Doreen lounged nearby, writing in a notebook with a weary demeanor. Gwen then interacted with her phone, mentioning a 'special family' dinner, before the scene shifted to the futuristic recreational space where the Girl with long black hair, Gwen, discussed 'the event' and 'three smiley faces' with stress, while the Woman with long dark hair and yellow glasses inquired about a 'family event' and the Woman with the denim jacket and glasses humorously listed symptoms. Gwen then turned to a sleeping orange dog, 'Mushface,' who grunted and snorted, and upon her touch, yawned loudly, revealing a small, green-furred squirrel with a yellow bow tie inside its mouth. The squirrel's frantic squeaking caused a cloud of white smoke to erupt from the dog's mouth, startling Gwen, and the Woman with long dark hair identified the dog as 'Lockjaw,' noting his 'uncontrollable teleportations' as a symptom, visually confirmed by a 'poof' sound. The Boy with reddish-brown hair appeared concerned, and a new character, the Girl with blonde hair and pink tips, wearing a white crop top with a pink spider emblem, observed sternly before exclaiming 'Tyne!' as the squirrel continued its sounds.

The Girl, whose name is Gwen, gestures as she speaks, expressing frustration, "That's the millionth text you've gotten today. What's going on?" The Boy, with a calm tone, dismisses it as "no, no more than usual, just, you know, nothing." Gwen responds, "Yeah. Seems like nothing," followed by another text notification. The Woman with long dark hair and yellow glasses, named Doreen, joins them, arms crossed and displaying a skeptical expression, while Gwen explains the texts are "just my mom. Since I joined the Secret Warriors, I keep missing family stuff. She's all over me." The Boy suggests, "it's good things are slow. The Boy questions, "That's kind of what this is, right?" Gwen then playfully reassures him, "Don't worry. Smiley face? Smiley face," adding, "Only two. Any more than that and she'll suspect something." The scene transitions as Gwen activates a device on her jacket, revealing a glowing red circular emblem with a lightning bolt, triggering a portal beneath them. They step into a futuristic, circular chamber with a red and white target design on the floor, and the portal closes. raw_segments: The minute opens in a city park at dusk, with a tense atmosphere underscored by subtle ambient sounds. Gabby, a girl with blonde hair and pink tips, appears concerned as she speaks to a serious-looking boy with reddish-brown hair. He sternly asks, "Why are you here, Gabby?", to which she firmly replies, "I'm not going back to Illinois without you. I mean it." The boy, intense and frustrated, retorts, "And I meant everything that I said last night. You admitted it. Mom and Dad aren't ready to forgive me." Gabby softly pleads, "Give them time," but he sharply insists, "Time won't fix this. You shouldn't have come here." Suddenly, a dramatic whoosh and explosion sound erupt as fire bursts behind the boy, causing him alarm. Tense, dramatic music swells as he then calmly conjures a small flame in his hand, holding it steadily. He coldly states, "See? Even you're scared of me," to which Gabby defensively begins, "No, I just..." before he cuts her off, declaring, "This is who I am. It's not safe to be around me." With a quiet, resigned tone, he tells her, "Just... go." The dramatic music continues, shifting to an ethereal, melancholic tone as Gabby approaches him, looking surprised by his control over the flame. A wide shot shows them walking away from the fire with the cityscape in the background. The scene then transitions to a brightly lit kitchen, accompanied by cheerful, lighthearted music. A woman with dark skin smiles as she stirs a large pot of vibrant orange stew. Gabby, now delighted, tastes the stew, exclaiming, "Mmm, chicken soup. I'm so hungry right now." The woman calmly warns, "I wouldn't," just as a small, squirrel-like creature with a yellow collar and silver medallion appears, making distinct squeaking and chittering sounds as it mischievously steals a spoonful of stew. Gabby looks shocked, then confused, stating, "I don't think that's chicken." Whimsical, mischievous music plays as the final shot reveals a large bag of dog food labeled 'YUM!' with a husky on the front, confirming the stew's true purpose, leading Gabby to exclaim, "Ew."

event_blocks.summary: Inside a warmly lit living room with abstract art and a blue couch, a female voice declared, "Bam! See that? It's too much traffic past the graveyard." The Red-haired man in black suit, initially smug, responded with a dismissive laugh, "That's because people are dying to get in there," before conceding, "You're right, we'll do the tunnel." His expression shifted to alarm as the Woman with dark skin and black sleeveless top confronted him, hands on her hips, adjusting her large triangular earring, and stated in a patronizing tone, "Sweetheart, I mean this in the most nurturing and maternal way. And we want you to do you. But there's nothing you can help us with," followed by an emphatic, "Really? Nothing!" The Woman with denim jacket and glasses entered from a doorway, her golden necklace with turquoise beads glowing as she conjured a small flame in her palm, exclaiming, "Mom, Dad, I have something I really want to show you!" and "This is really cool. If you just check it out and see what I can do and then maybe I can help you." The Red-haired man, with a dismissive and sarcastic male voice, cut her off, "Now, now, sweetie, Mommy and Daddy are doing important supervillain things," and later, "We love you anyway, blah blah blah, you're perfect the way you are." The Woman with denim jacket and glasses, frustrated, pleaded, "Will you please just listen to me for once!" The Woman with dark skin and black sleeveless top, maintaining dominance, pointed accusingly and sternly reprimanded, "Oh, not with that tone, young lady. Now go to your room!" The Red-haired man reacted with shock and defensive gestures to the flame, while the Woman with denim jacket and glasses stepped back, her expression shifting from focused to surprised, observing the confrontation with concern as her flame flickered. The Woman with dark skin and black sleeveless top, exasperated, muttered, "Ugh... I'll tell you the one power I wish she had. Being seen and not heard," to which the Red-haired man dismissively agreed, "Okay."

Then everyone will see me and hear me, loud and clear!" The man reacted with alarm to the crackling flames, while an unidentified male voice suggested, "How about grabbing dinner when we finish? I am craving barbecue." The scene shifted to a futuristic recreational space where a red-haired woman in a denim jacket and brown hat, with a squirrel-like creature named Tap, reviewed surveillance footage on a laptop showing the living room incident. She explained, "I tweaked my facial recognition program to overlay onto social media feeds. We'll find her. I'd have to pat myself on the back, mostly because my arms are kind of on the short side." A girl with long black hair in a denim jacket and red scarf joined her, and a boy with reddish-brown hair in a black hoodie with a red flame emblem appeared, pointing accusingly at the girl. As the group, including a blonde woman with pink hair tips, gathered around the laptop, the girl with long black hair stated, "I'm just gonna go see what's up." Sounds of punching and grunting were heard, followed by the boy exclaiming, "Punching bag! Nice. Very Captain America." The red-haired woman then gestured toward a circular platform and climbing wall, and the boy pointed, indicating a plan. The boy's expression turned intense as he clenched his fists, expressing frustration, "I hate that I can't help when it's my powers causing the trouble." The girl reassured him, "Last I checked, you're not the one blowing up ice cream carts. And you are helping. As much as any of us right now." Later, in the same futuristic space, the Boy with reddish-brown hair confronted the Girl with long black hair, accusingly asking, "No! No!" The Girl reassured him, "Look, we'll figure this out and get your powers back and you'll be toasting marshmallows again in no time." The Boy softened, saying, "You're amazing," then corrected himself, "I mean, uh, you guys. All you guys." The Girl challenged him, "What do you mean 'you'? The team is all of us, even without your... Unless you don't want them back." The Boy, conflicted, explained he didn't want Zayla to have the powers but also didn't want to leave his new life, school, and friends, concluding, "I don't want to leave... everyone." The Girl reacted with surprise, "Leave? You're actually thinking about that? You promised not to keep anything big from me, remember? And this would be big." The Boy somberly acknowledged, "Yeah, it would be." During their tense conversation, a fast-paced female announcer voice from a game on a large screen declared, "Major update!
